title: Issues Tonight — Rankett dev rolling log + Speen eulogy
html:
TL;DR

  • A rolling stream-of-issues doc Yahir uses as a working notepad for fixing rankett tool UX — Tab 15 through Tab 22, dozens of micro-fixes between Dec 2025 and Jan 2026.
  • Mid-doc, Yahir drops a memorial paragraph for "Speen" (a friend who died) — it sits inside the dev log as if grief and product-iteration share the same surface.
  • Core technical thread: location-based query relevance is broken (every prompt jams the city/state in, when natural-language client lookups are sometimes local, sometimes general); needs a brand-reach screen during onboarding.
  • SaaS pivot logic surfaces: under-$5K/mo revenue → "buy tool" CTA, $5K+ → book-a-call funnel, gated by Discord-sourced elijah leach copy.
  • Long intake-form draft for fully white-labeled rankett onboarding: Google Business Profile, website access, social-media handler, content surface inventory, AI tone-of-voice preference.

The dev log

Through Dec 2025 and into Jan 2026, Yahir kept appending issue clusters by "Tab" — Tab 15, 21, 22 — each a different Codex/Claude-Code session he was running in parallel. Recurring frustrations:

  • The overview tab gauge + radar/spider chart feels "cheap and shitty" — needs to feel like a real SaaS dashboard, not a pile of cards.
  • Hardcoded values in the overview — high-impact-fixes count, position number — possibly not real; would mean the tool isn't actually working, which is the existential dread underneath the cosmetic complaints.
  • Email-capture flow is unclear — where does the email go after submission? Capture is likely happening but not verifiable.
  • Query naturalness — the prompts the tool feeds the AI are jamming city+state into every query, but a real client lookup ("best coffee shop near me") wouldn't always include geography. Wants a brand-reach scoping screen (worldwide / national / regional / state / city / neighborhood) during onboarding.
  • Competitors tab needs a fully revamp — searchable, scraped competitors, drag-rank top 3, type-in slots 4 and 5.
  • Loading screens must match aesthetic between first and second pass.
  • Drag-animation ghost issue on competitor selection — feels disattached.
  • ROI tab error: "Application area, client side exception has occurred while loading tool.dot RankettAI."
  • Blog automation + Reddit/Quora warmed accounts per key region.
  • Point-in-time → ongoing audits: weekly automatic re-runs, PDF reports auto-emailed even to lukewarm leads who only entered an email once.

The Speen paragraph

Mid-doc, Yahir writes:

"So many memories and good goofy times Speen. From filming trickshots for hours, to fishing at the 'Old People' spot illegally, to having to gameplan around you on the paintball course because of how truly reckless and fearless you were, to you visiting me at OSU and getting lost looking for a chicken fried steak that you so deeply desired, you were 1 of 1 pal. I wince and still genuinely can't believe it, but I find solace in remembering your energy and how EVERY SINGLE TIME we saw each other we knew we were going to make each other laugh one way or another. Cheers eternally brother, forever my brother."

It sits inside a TODO list as if grief and ongoing iteration share the same workspace. This is consistent with as a behavior pattern — Yahir doesn't compartmentalize emotionally; both the dev sprint and the eulogy live in the same doc. Adds speen to the entity graph as a grants pass teen era friend now lost.

SaaS pivot decision

Underwritten in the doc: prospects who report <$5K/mo revenue get the buy-the-tool button (self-serve SaaS path); $5K+ get the call-booking funnel with multiple touchpoints and a closer message from "Didles" — likely a rankett internal alias. This gates the funnel by client-economic-fit, preserving sales bandwidth.

Intake form (Rankett onboarding)

The latter half of the doc is a draft intake-form spec covering: Google Business Profile ownership/access, website CMS access patterns, social/marketing handler, asset inventory (blog, YouTube, podcast, FB, LinkedIn, Yelp, industry directories), preferred handoff method (add-as-user / DIY / agency-handles / help-needed), AI tone-of-voice (professional / friendly / direct), and explicit "don't say" guardrails. This is the operationalization of ghost white label — the form is what makes Rankett actually run on the agency's behalf.

Privacy

No credentials in this doc. Speen's full name not used in raw — kept as nickname. didles / "Ddidles" appears as a Rankett collaborator alias.

related.label: Rankett context handoff — separating internal tool from white-label
